pull.title: ipc: fix exception type visibility with REDUCE_EXPORTS on macOS
pull.body:
Fixes #34723.

On macOS with `REDUCE_EXPORTS`, Cap'n Proto RPC reports `remote exception: unknown non-KJ exception of type: kj::Exception` instead of the actual exception message. This happens because `type-context.h` re-throws a stored `kj::Exception` with a raw `throw` from bitcoin-node code, where `kj::Exception` typeinfo has hidden visibility (due to `REDUCE_EXPORTS` setting `-fvisibility=hidden`). The hidden typeinfo in the executable is not coalesced with `libkj.dylib`'s copy by the macOS dynamic linker, so `getCaughtExceptionAsKj()`'s `catch (kj::Exception& e)` fails and falls through to `catch (...)`.

The fix replaces `throw kj::mv(*exception)` with `kj::throwRecoverableException(kj::mv(*exception))`, which routes the throw through `libkj.dylib` where it gets wrapped in `ExceptionImpl` with consistent typeinfo. This is the same pattern Cap'n Proto uses internally in `kj/async-inl.h` when re-throwing stored exceptions from promises.

Also removes the test workaround added in 8fe91f3719 that accepted the degraded error message on Darwin.

**Tested:** `interface_ipc_mining.py` passes on macOS (arm64) with `REDUCE_EXPORTS=ON`. Verified that without this change, the test fails with the exact error described in the issue.

The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers.

<!--021abf342d371248e50ceaed478a90ca-->
### Reviews
See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process.
A summary of reviews will appear here.
<!--5faf32d7da4f0f540f40219e4f7537a3-->

Marking as draft. The `type-context.h` change needs to go through libmultiprocess upstream first
(bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess#268). Once that's merged, I'll update this PR with a subtree merge + the test cleanup.

I you want fully green CI, you could do a subtree update as the first commit. Though the fact that the other CI jobs pass should be enough to allow review of https://github.com/bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess/pull/268.
I closed #34977 in favor of this PR, since you now include both the subtree update and a test for one of the updates.
Reopened with a subtree merge from bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess#268 + the test workaround removal.

One little gotcha is that (when possible) subtree updates need to be based on the commit of the last update. So you'll want to rebase this branch back onto 613a548648880314e78c3045d0ded12e29a4f036.

Alternatively you can start from my branch )[2026/04/libmultiprocess-subtree](https://github.com/Sjors/bitcoin/tree/2026/04/libmultiprocess-subtree) and cherry-pick your test commit on it.

events.body: Mmm, CI is not running, maybe the test commit interacts with something on master?

events.message:
test: remove macOS REDUCE_EXPORTS exception workaround

The underlying issue was fixed in bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess#268.

Remove the workaround that accepted degraded error messages on Darwin.
